# dreamd

> dreamd makes Claude Code, Cursor, and Cline remember the same things. Drop a
> `.agent/` folder in your repo, run `npx dreamd-mcp`, and every MCP-aware coding
> agent reads and writes the same memory — checked into git alongside your code.
>
> AGENTS.md is what you wrote down. dreamd is what your agent learned, across every tool.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/README.md): Overview, install, and quick start
- [SPEC.md](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/SPEC.md): `.agent/` folder layout, JSON schema, salience formula, dream-cycle contract
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Project context for coding agents working in this repo
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Dev setup and contribution guide
- [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Threat model and disclosure policy
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history

## Adapters

- [Claude Code](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/adapters/claude-code/.mcp.json.example): `.mcp.json` config for Claude Code
- [Cursor](https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd/blob/main/adapters/cursor/README.md): MCP config and agent recall rule for Cursor

## Package

- [npm: dreamd-mcp](https://www.npmjs.com/package/dreamd-mcp): `npx dreamd-mcp` — downloads the right prebuilt binary, no Rust required
